description_long: This structure is a traditional Chinese pagoda, featuring a circular, multi-tiered roof with distinct upward-curving eaves. The central building is elevated on a broad, octagonal stone platform with concentric steps leading up to the main entrance. The roof is dark gray and layered, topped with a decorative finial at the apex. The walls of the pagoda are a rich reddish-brown, punctuated by columns and arched doorways, while the surrounding platform is a muted gray stone. A secondary, rectangular building with a blue-tiled roof and red walls is attached to one side, likely serving as an entrance or auxiliary hall. The overall design is ornate and symmetrical, with architectural details characteristic of imperial Chinese religious structures.
description: A circular, multi-tiered Chinese pagoda with upward-curving eaves, set on a broad stone platform and featuring a decorative finial and attached auxiliary hall.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the main pagoda's circular structure, layered roof, and the broad stone steps leading up to the entrance.
description_view_1: This side view reveals the attached rectangular auxiliary building with a blue roof and the full extent of the octagonal platform.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the back entrance and the symmetry of the stone steps and platform, with the pagoda's roof tiers clearly visible.
description_view_3: This angled view emphasizes the connection between the main pagoda and the auxiliary building, as well as the layered roof structure.
